name: Route from Castejón de Monegros to Cart
name_original: De Castejón de Monegros a Cartuja de Las Fuentes
description: Cycle 28.6 miles through the Monegros semi-desert from Castejón de Monegros to the historic Cartuja de Las Fuentes, a moderate route.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Castejón De Monegros traverse a distinctive semi-desert landscape characterized by arid terrain and striking ochre colors. The region is nestled at the base of the eastern foothills of the Sierra de Alcubierre, offering varied elevations. Unique geological features like the Tozales de Monegros, sculpted by erosion, define much of the scenery. This environment provides a unique backdrop for cycling, blending open plains with resilient local flora. Best touring cycling routes around Castejón De Monegros The most popular touring cycling route is Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena, a 51.8 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 7 hours 10 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers extensive views across the Monegros landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monegros, the Spanish Western — circular gravel from the Monastery of Sigena, a moderate 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route features gravel sections and passes by historical sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena, a 15.7 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Laguna de Sariñena, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Castejón De Monegros is defined by semi-desert landscapes, arid plains, and unique ochre rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more demanding routes with significant elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Castejón De Monegros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Castejón De Monegros?
A: Castejón De Monegros offers a diverse selection of nearly 20 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 2 easy, 11 moderate, and 5 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for almost every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Castejón De Monegros?
A: The terrain in Castejón De Monegros is characterized by its unique semi-desert landscape, featuring arid plains and striking ochre colors. You'll encounter a mix of well-maintained paved sections and unpaved segments, particularly on gravel biking trails. Routes often traverse the eastern foothills of the Sierra de Alcubierre, offering varied elevations and geological features like the Tozales de Monegros.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Castejón De Monegros?
A: While cycling, you can explore several distinctive natural landmarks. Keep an eye out for the fascinating multi-colored rock formations known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, sculpted by centuries of erosion. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit)&lt;/a&gt; is a recognizable stone monolith serving as a landmark. Some routes also pass by the contrasting natural element of the Laguna de Sariñena.

Q: Are there historical or cultural sites along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You might encounter the well-preserved Castle-Hermitage of San Sebastián and San Fabián, dating back to 1211, or the 13th-century Parish Church of Nuestra Señora de la Lumbre. The town also features historic stately homes, known as Casa Solariegas, and 'La Madre' Fountain, an ancient water source with potential Arab origins.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Castejón De Monegros?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379793520/from-castejon-de-monegros-to-miramon-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Castejón de Monegros to Miramón — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.8 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980280092/laguna-de-sarinena-circular-from-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25.4 km trail.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Castejón De Monegros?
A: The Monegros region is characterized by high temperatures, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling. During these times, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable rides. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Castejón De Monegros offers routes suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980280092/laguna-de-sarinena-circular-from-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 25.4 km trail that leads through varied terrain and is often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes, making it a good option for less experienced cyclists.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379789762/vuelta-al-monegros-circular-desde-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 83.3 km route with significant elevation changes, offering extensive views across the Monegros landscape. It typically takes over 7 hours to complete.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling trips?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the region provides options that can extend your touring cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379789762/vuelta-al-monegros-circular-desde-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 83.3 km route, offering a full day's adventure. You can also combine shorter routes to create multi-day excursions.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Castejón De Monegros?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Castejón De Monegros, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the unique semi-desert landscapes, the striking ochre colors, and the sense of adventure provided by the varied terrain. The well-documented routes and the opportunity to explore both natural and historical sites are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, Castejón de Monegros, as a base for outdoor activities, typically offers parking options within the town or near popular starting points for routes. It's advisable to check local information or maps for designated parking zones before your trip.

Q: Can I find routes that include gravel sections?
A: Yes, the Monegros region is known for its gravel biking opportunities. A notable route that includes gravel sections is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971333335/monegros-the-spanish-western-circular-gravel-from-the-monastery-of-sigena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monegros, the Spanish Western — circular gravel from the Monastery of Sigena&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 39.9 km path offers a mix of terrain and passes by historical sites.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the routes for scenic stops?
A: Absolutely. The region's unique landscape offers several points for scenic stops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; is a natural monument providing panoramic 360-degree views of the Monegros landscape. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bujal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot to take in the expansive scenery.
tips:
The Charterhouse of Our Lady of the Fountains, also known as the Charterhouse of Monegros, is a former monastery located in the municipality of Sariñena, in the region of Monegros, province of Huesca, Aragon. Founded in 1507 by the Counts of Sástago, Beatriz de Luna, and Blasco de Aragón, it is the first Charterhouse built in the Kingdom of Aragon.

The current monastery, built in the 18th century, is an impressive architectural complex in the late Baroque style. It is notable for its mural paintings by Friar Manuel Bayeu, Goya's brother-in-law, depicting scenes from the life of the Virgin Mary, women of the Old Testament, and the cardinal virtues.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the touring bicycle route From Castejón de Monegros to Cartuja de Las Fuentes?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While not overly technical, it covers approximately 46 km with about 430 meters of elevation gain, requiring a reasonable level of physical preparation. Some sections may be unpaved and challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain typical of the Monegros region. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including quiet asphalt, gravel, and potentially some rougher, unpaved sections or rutted tracks. A robust touring bike capable of handling mixed surfaces is highly recommended for a comfortable ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete the 46 km route in about 2 hours and 50 minutes. However, this duration can vary depending on your pace, the type of bike you're riding, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route features unique Monegros landscapes and culminates at the impressive Charterhouse of Our Lady of Las Fuentes. You'll also pass by the Castle-Hermitage of San Sebastián. The Cartuja is a significant historical and artistic site, known for its extensive 18th-century frescoes.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, varied terrain, and some potentially challenging unpaved sections, this route is generally not recommended for absolute beginners or young children. Cyclists should have a good level of fitness and experience with mixed-surface riding.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Monegros region can experience extreme temperatures. The best times to cycle are typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Castejón de Monegros. You should be able to find suitable parking within the village to begin your cycling tour. Specific parking areas for cyclists are usually available near the town center or public facilities.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on the general trail, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in rural areas where livestock may be present. If you plan to visit the Charterhouse of Our Lady of Las Fuentes, check their specific pet policies as historical sites often have restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the route itself. However, if you wish to take a guided tour of the Charterhouse of Our Lady of Las Fuentes, there may be an entrance fee. Guided tours are typically offered on weekends and national holidays.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section of this route, approximately 7%, overlaps with the Camino Natural de Castejón de Monegros. This means you'll briefly share a path that is part of a larger network of natural trails.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the distinctive semi-desert landscape of Monegros, characterized by arid plains, rolling hills, and ravines. Look out for unique geological formations like the 'torrollones' and resilient vegetation adapted to dry conditions. The route offers expansive views, potentially including the distant Sierra de Alcubierre.

waypoints.name: Charterhouse of Our Lady of Las Fuentes
